title: aptamer_nanobot_cross_v1 — CROSS-AXIS F2 output rows
description: Output schema for _python_bridge/module/aptamer_nanobot_cross.py. CROSS-AXIS integration F2: an aptamer is a functional (binding) element embedded in a DNA nanostructure (e.g. an aptamer-gated cargo cage), so the APTAMER sub-axis's ligand-binding equilibrium IS the NANOBOT core-5 axis's actuation trigger. For each device in a deterministic aptamer-gated-nanodevice panel, the APTAMER sub-axis (aptamer_affinity_sim.py, imported verbatim — no fork) folds the embedded aptamer (Nussinov 1978 bp-max + Turner-style NN stack-sum dG) and computes the 1:1 Langmuir fraction-bound theta at the trigger-ligand concentration; theta is mapped through a gating threshold to the NANOBOT axis's phi(6)=2 binary actuator state (OPEN/CLOSED). The aptamer Kd values are literature-informed illustrative magnitudes; the theta->OPEN/CLOSED mapping is a modeling choice. In-silico simulator-consistency only (AGENTS.tape g8/f2) — NOT a structural / binding-affinity / nanodevice-function / therapeutic / regulatory claim. No fold dG / Kd / theta / count is derived from the n=6 lattice (g2/f_lattice_fit). A cross-axis bridge is NOT a new axis: the hexa-bio core-5 set (QUANTUM, WEAVE, NANOBOT, RIBOZYME, VIROCAPSID) is UNCHANGED.

device_id.description: Identifier of the aptamer-gated DNA nanodevice from the deterministic device panel.

device_note.description: Illustrative note describing the gated nanodevice and its trigger condition.

aptamer_name.description: Name of the aptamer embedded as the binding/trigger element, from the APTAMER sub-axis corpus.

aptamer_sequence.description: Aptamer sequence (RNA alphabet; DNA T folded to U by the APTAMER sub-axis).

trigger_ligand.description: The ligand whose presence triggers the aptamer fold and thereby actuates the nanodevice.

aptamer_paper_ref.description: Literature reference for the embedded aptamer (from the APTAMER sub-axis corpus).

fold_dot_bracket.description: Nussinov 1978 bp-max fold of the aptamer in dot-bracket notation.

fold_num_base_pairs.description: Number of base pairs in the Nussinov fold.

folding_free_energy_kcal_per_mol.description: Turner-style NN stack-sum folding free energy of the binding-competent pocket (kcal/mol); <= 0 (stabilizing or neutral).

fold_model.description: APTAMER sub-axis fold model string (Nussinov 1978 + Turner-style NN stack sum).

binding_model.description: APTAMER sub-axis binding model string — must reference the 1:1 Langmuir equilibrium.

kd_nM.description: Aptamer dissociation constant Kd (nM). Literature-informed illustrative magnitude.

koff_s.description: Dissociation rate constant koff (s^-1); Kd = koff / kon.

trigger_ligand_x_kd.description: Trigger-ligand concentration expressed as a multiple of the aptamer's own Kd.

fraction_bound_theta.description: 1:1 Langmuir fraction-bound theta = [L]/(Kd+[L]) at the trigger-ligand concentration. Equals 0.5 exactly when trigger_ligand_x_kd == 1.

gating_theta_threshold.description: Gating threshold on theta: the NANOBOT actuator is OPEN iff theta >= this. Illustrative fixed value, NOT lattice-derived.

nanobot_axis_source.description: Path to the NANOBOT core axis source whose phi(6)=2 binary actuator-output declaration is read as structural text (actuation logic untouched — f3).

nanobot_axis_role.description: Role of the NANOBOT core-5 axis (DNA-nanotechnology actuation).

actuator_output_declared_by_axis.description: The phi(6)=2 binary actuator-output (bound vs unbound / open-closed clamp) the NANOBOT axis declares for itself — read as structural text, never used as a derivation source.

actuator_open.description: True iff theta >= gating_theta_threshold — the aptamer binding equilibrium drives the NANOBOT phi(6)=2 actuator OPEN.

actuator_state.description: Binary NANOBOT actuator state — OPEN iff actuator_open is true.

kcat_per_s.description: Always 0.0 — an aptamer is a non-catalytic binder (the defining distinction from the parent RIBOZYME axis).

in_silico_caveat.description: Restatement that every row is in-silico simulator-consistency only (g8/f2).

illustrative_only.description: Always true — the Kd values and the theta->OPEN/CLOSED mapping are illustrative; the row is not a prediction of real nanodevice gating behaviour.
